title: salix aurita var. grandifolia
intro: Salix aurita var. grandifolia, also known as the large-leaved willow, is a variant of the Salix aurita species, a type of willow tree native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The large-leaved willow has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Salix aurita var. grandifolia can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in natural medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
